page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage volumes. There are no integrated GPUs, and the configuration includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The high memory density relative to the vCPU count provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require substantial memory footprints without demanding excessive processing power. This resource balance makes the instance appropriate for memory-intensive applications such as caching layers, small-scale databases, and enterprise applications that require a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with a single thread per core, providing predictable compute performance. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute density. The ecs.m2.medium is designed for workloads such as in-memory caching, database hosting, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m2.medium is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization with dedicated CPU allocation
Zero local storage, requiring external block storage volumes
Designed for in-memory caching, databases, and memory-bound applications
